review_title: 6+ Years of Reliable Use – A Framework You Can Truly Trust
description: Our overall experience with ABP has been outstanding over the past 6+ years. What gives us the most confidence is how consistently the team keeps the framework up to date. Regular updates, new features, and timely security patches show that this is a product that is actively maintained and improved. We never feel like we are stuck with outdated technology. It has brought tremendous comfort and reliability to our development process, and we trust it completely for our production applications.
author: Huseyin S.
pros: We have been using ABP for over 6 years now, and it has truly transformed the way we develop software. The framework is incredibly well-structured and saves us an enormous amount of time. The modular architecture, built-in best practices, and ready-to-use infrastructure components make our daily development work so much easier and more efficient. It has become an indispensable part of our tech stack.
cons: The initial learning curve can be a bit steep for new developers joining the team. Understanding the full extent of the framework and its conventions takes some time. However, once you get past that stage, everything becomes very intuitive and well worth the investment.

review_title: Fantastic platform. If you are not using it for your development projects you should be
description: The value for money that we get from the plafrom and ROI is unprecendented.
author: Peter B.
pros: ABP commercial allows us to kick start any new project by at least 6 months. We dont worry about starting from the beginning we start creating customer needs from day one as the framework provides us such a kick start.
cons: Some of the releases that are happening, while frequent are still leaving some key features on the backload. e.g. WebHooks, CQRS etc.

review_title: Strong guardrails for building serious SaaS systems
description: I’ve genuinely enjoyed using ABP. For me, the biggest value is that it removes a lot of low-value decisions and forces some discipline in how you structure a SaaS system. •Value for Money: High. Especially if you are building a multi-tenant SaaS with enterprise requirements •Customer Support: Good. Generally responsive, though sometimes you have to dig deeper yourself •Ease of Use: Moderate. There is a learning curve because of the conventions •Customization Options: Good. You can extend it, but only if you understand how things are wired internally •Code Management: Strong. It pushes you towards clean layering and modular thinking •Bugs and Issues: Low to moderate. Stable overall, occasional friction in edge cases •Cloud Services: Neutral. You have to bring your own cloud-native thinking
author: Vibhor M.
pros: •It gives you opinionated guardrails. That matters a lot today when agentic AI development can go in too many directions •Multi-tenancy, auth, permissions, auditing are not afterthoughts. They are built in •It helps contain implementation chaos. Things have a place •Good fit for teams that want to build serious SaaS, not just ship features quickly •Reduces long-term architectural drift if you stay aligned with it
cons: The initial setup does take a little while to get a hang of. The customer support was responsive and was able to help me set it all up on a Mac.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Microservice and DevOps CI/CD Pipeline need to support more. Besides, no well ready containers image can be found. E.g. Jenkins container image to build this project, runtime container image to ready for this project, CI/CD pipeline script to deploy the container image. In my company environment, it cannot download npm javascript package but I try to fix it a long time ago without anyone support.
author: Tim Y.
pros: Multi-trier, SaaS and Microservice template. However, the learning curve is too long.
cons: This framework provide an UI suite and easy to generate different template but lack of support the update the existing project.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Dependable software with a great dashboard
description: I made this software my secret tool. I find it amazing so far.
author: Violet R.
pros: I was looking for a reliable software. ABP gave it all. It involves effortless software development and it is straight forward. The dashboard is awesome. I can easily customize it.
cons: There is no cons in my experience. I like ABP Commercial. Their help desk is quite impressive.

review_title: A Well-Structured Solution for Efficient Development
description: ABP is a powerful framework that simplifies application development and maintenance. With its robust architecture and prebuilt configurations, it provides an efficient and structured approach to software development. Despite some areas for improvement, such as automated upgrades, ABP remains a highly recommended framework for developers seeking a streamlined and scalable solution.
author: Merdan G.
pros: ABP provides a comprehensive foundation for application development, integrating essential features such as permissions, identity modules, and feature management right out of the box. This preconfiguration significantly reduces development overhead and ensures consistency. The framework's adherence to Domain-Driven Design (DDD) architecture is another highlight, offering a well-structured project setup that promotes scalability and maintainability.
cons: While ABP streamlines development in many ways, an automated upgrade system for new versions would greatly enhance its usability. Keeping up with updates manually can be time-consuming, and having a built-in upgrade mechanism would ensure a smoother transition between versions.

description: It was really great, ABP Suite and Studio are best in class tools for development.
author: Kaushik G.
pros: It’s both Monolithic and Microsevices architecture gives us the opportunity to scale our applications even more.
cons: Angular experience is ok. Need other fronted such as React.

review_title: Solid foundation for serious product development
description: ABP Suite has come into it's own, it is now a serious productivity enhancer. Architecturally sound, ABP provides a solid foundation for all of our current projects.
author: Karim A.
pros: 5* architecture, the ABP team are rockstar developers and they know exactly what they are doing. I use their code as a benchmark for what my team writes in many cases. Nice to see the new MAUI client being developed, which is closing the gap from the old XAMARIN implementation on the previous platform. All in all the release cycles have got much better, bugs are being fixed faster and new features are being brought in without too many issues.
cons: The ABP team, being rockstar developers, can sometimes make things hard for entry level / mid level devs to get up to speed with the platform. Documentation has got *much* better over the past couple of years, but I think there is still a ways to go before you can let a JR Dev loose on the platform without any guidance.

description: Overall, we have been very happy with the suite once getting over the initial learning curve. We are using it as the foundation of a SAAS product which is being built to replace a 40 year old on-prem solution.
author: Chris R.
pros: The ABP framework allows your team to skip the coding of all of the basic, foundational code required for a web application, and go straight to the business logic that will power your app. It also provides a skeleton that follows best practices to help keep your code base easily maintainable as it grows.
cons: There is a steep learning curve to understanding the 'ABP' way of doing things (although it does pay dividends once you understand it). The support can sometimes be a challenge. Some of the support staff are excellent, but there are a couple who seem to always give an answer that reads along the lines of "read the help".

review_title: Saving time for common basic features but customizable
description: Creates the base code and gives me time to focus attention on the specific code of the solution to be developed
author: Emanuele F.
pros: many basic features ready to usepowerfull extendibility to customizewell documentedABP Suite usefull on initial design / code generation
cons: some module missing for (newer) Blazor / MauiABP Suite need to be improved for code review during developmentBlazorise controls dependant

review_title: Out-of-the-box features saving a lot of money and time
description: We love ABP. We don't have to write everything from scratch. We start from out-of-the-box features and just focus on what we really need to write.
author: Yuttana B.
pros: ABP has many common features out-of-the-box that we can use right away. By using these features as our starter code, we are able to save a lot of time, effect and money. Also ABP is well-architected and the code is high quality with less bugs. If we would have to write everything we needed by our own, we might have to spend years. Once more things we like is that the new version, or issue fixing or improvement come out very soon every other weeks. We don't to wait too long.
cons: In few cases, we need to customize thing, however we figure out that the document related to that is still under construction. Also between major version, there are some breaking changes. However, it is completely understandable and acceptable since we still can ask support.

description: we are totally already have satisfied of what ABP offer, we already using it in more than 3 applications
author: Osama A.
pros: the structure of the solution and ready to go modules
cons: Themes need to be enhanced, the documentation for the commercial edition it should has its own version, now the documentation is mixed with the free version

description: Excellent. It allowed us to pivot our business from traditional software development to SaaS.
author: Gerard C.
pros: ABP allowed us to pivot from being a traditional software vendor to being a true SaaS provider. We were able to make the pivot using our existing development team which had very limited SaaS development experience. ABP allowed us to get up and running very quickly by providing the foundation we could build on.
cons: We did not have any SaaS development skills so the learning curve was very steep. There are not a great deal of resources available to make that process easier. But there are enough to make it possible.

review_title: Great Development Framework to Standardize the Solution
author: Ying Kit L.
pros: ABP Commercial offers the solution that comes with operational ready administration module, system services and reusable frameworks for both frontend and backend components that we could leverage to rollout our projects in shorten turnaround time. In addition, ABP Commercial continues to enhance its existing framework, launch new modules/solutions based on the latest trends, and issue security patches when its solutions are at risk.
cons: Documentations may not be up to dated or comprehensive enough where my team needs time to read through the source codes.

description: Overall the framework has a lot of great features making development quick and easy.
author: Ryan R.
pros: You can quickly develop applications without worrying about building the core.
cons: Changing application theme can be challenging and some of the commercial modules like file management not fully ready with user access rights.

author: Kaushik B.
pros: Pre-built template was easy to setup and has multiple tech stack configurations. Well-documented and we use many of its core features in our applications.
cons: Documentation could be improved with more examples. Customization of framework wasn't easy at first to learn and more documentation on this would help. Some of the npm/nuget packages require diving into the source code to understand how it works and documentation on this would help.

description: write little code, mainly CRUD operations, but I miss a better screen layout
author: Jurenville J.
pros: Architeture is ready. DDD. focus in business
cons: could be more options of themes and components

review_title: ABP not only helps us boost up project initialization but also saves a lot of efforts
description: So far so good. It can help us boost up the project structure initialization
author: Hoa T.
pros: Strong infrastructure skeletons Rich of template options
cons: Licensing model is not suitable for big team. It should be based on ABP Suite using slots (who can run Abp Suite tool)

author: Burak M.
pros: fast application development and easy use.
cons: Version updates are very fast. we can't catch up.

author: Grant J.
pros: The toolset is feature rich and allows my team to focus on business features as oppose to codebase scaffolding.
cons: You should have a strong technical capable team. Onboarding junior developers on project requires allot of attention for they will get lost quick and burn time.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: ABP products are a system that helps me and my team produce high-quality and reliable solutions at industry standards very quickly.
author: Hüseyin Ç.
pros: The system structure and code quality generated by ABP are quite successful.
cons: It would be better if there were more theme options.

description: In my abp project, I mainly use the standard version to split the original old project from a single project to a saas product. Among them, I have to praise abp's architectural thinking on DDD. The division of each module allows us to quickly iteratively develop according to customer customized needs without affecting the main body of the company's saas products, increasing development efficiency.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: ABP Commercial has many complete sets of module products. Each module is designed to be very standard and reasonable, and is easy for secondary development, rapid iteration, and can be used out of the box. Most modules meet market standard business functions and also support multi-platform system architecture such as windows/Linux/Mac. Among the commercial modules, I like Lepton
cons: Although abp suite can quickly generate pages, it does not support generating complex business forms. For example, in a complex system because there are many relationships with complex table structures, it cannot be well supported. Finally, I would like to make some suggestions. In the table structure association, I hope to add an option to associate the entities of any module in the solution with new entities.

review_title: The ABP Framework is a powerful option for developers who want to develop web applications that are modern, scalable, and easy to maintain.
description: ABP suite is definitely a useful and very powerful tool. It is very simple to integrate with the development environment and add and remove modules.
author: Emrullah I.
pros: The ABP Framework offers the opportunity to develop modern, scalable and easy-to-maintain web applications.
cons: During the time I used ABP Framework Commercial, there was no situation that I did not like.

author: Eric U.
pros: Every platform has its quirks and abp is no different; but if you want a solid, well thought-out platform to give you a head start on all of the enterprise-software bits that you're gonna need anyway, this is great stuff.
cons: The tooling can occasionally be flaky, and there are gotchas: *never* accidentally create app service methods that produce duplicate api endpoints, for example. Swagger will tell you that it's invalid, but if you don't think to look there first, you'll end up with thread pool starvation after authentication. Who knew?

review_title: A Solid foundation and building block for your software product
description: ABP Frameworks saved us thousands of development hours, most of the effort has been invested in working on the business problem we are trying to solve, instead of technical and infrastructure code.You won't agree with all the design choices the ABP team has made - it is, after all, an opinionated framework - yet you will be OK with a good portion of them.For new teams, proper planning of ABP learning is necessary; don't assume you are ready once you have created a couple of samples based on the provided templates and tutorials.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: An excellent and complete Full-Stack framework that applies industry best practices and solid design patterns, very-well integrated from your persistence code upward to your UI, and does an outstanding job of addressing numerous cross-cutting concerns in a transparent and reliable manner.
cons: There is a steep learning curve with ABP that can be lowered by producing more learning material; and more cross-layer function/feature focused material.In any serious large-scale software development, you will need at times to customize, tweak or even replace some of the the built-in functionality of your infrastructure and framework code to fit your unique requirements; and unfortunately, this is where things with ABP becomes the most complicated; Better documentation and resources on ABP internals are essential - This is a must! As I said this is the case for all serious software projects; no exception.
